title: Variables 3
description: This exercise explores uninitialized variables in Rust. The compiler prevents using variables that might not have a value.
instructions: Fix the code so that it compiles by ensuring the variable is initialized before use.
hints: The variable x is declared but never given a value.
Rust does not allow using uninitialized variables.
Assign a value to x: let x: i32 = 42;
explanation: Rust prevents the use of uninitialized variables at compile time. This is a safety feature that eliminates an entire class of bugs common in C and C++. Every variable must have a value before it can be read.
